phrases.intent_en: Ask an usher where your seats are
phrases.text_local: Excusez-moi, pouvez-vous m'indiquer mes places ? J'ai les billets rang G, sièges quinze et seize.
phrases.pronunciation: ex-KYOO-zay mwah, poo-VAY voo man-dee-KAY may PLASS ? zhay lay bee-YAY RAHN zhay, SYEZH KANZ ay SEZ.
phrases.cultural_note: At French theaters and opera houses, ushers ('les ouvreuses') traditionally escort you to your seat and expect a small tip — a charming tradition that dates back centuries.

responses.french: Suivez-moi, je vous accompagne à vos places.
responses.english: Follow me, I'll accompany you to your seats.
responses.phonetic: swee-VAY mwah, zhuh vooz ah-koh-PAHN-yuh ah voh PLASS.

responses.french: C'est dans la corbeille, au premier balcon à gauche.
responses.english: It's in the dress circle, on the first balcony to the left.
responses.phonetic: say dahn lah kor-BAY, oh pruh-MYAY bal-KOHN ah GOHSH.

phrases.text_local: J'adore l'opéra. C'est une passion depuis mon enfance.
phrases.pronunciation: zhah-DOR loh-pay-RAH. say toon pah-SYON duh-PWEE mohn ahn-FAHNS.
phrases.cultural_note: Opera is woven deeply into French culture — Bizet, Berlioz, and Massenet are national treasures. Expressing a love of opera will immediately endear you to French speakers.

phrases.intent_en: Order a glass of champagne at intermission
phrases.text_local: Une coupe de champagne, s'il vous plaît.
phrases.pronunciation: oon KOOP duh shahm-PAH-nyuh, seel voo PLAY.
phrases.cultural_note: At the opera, you order 'une coupe' (a coupe glass) of champagne — not 'un verre' (a regular glass). The distinction marks you as someone who knows their way around a theater foyer.

responses.french: Bien sûr. Vous préférez du brut ou du demi-sec ?
responses.english: Of course. Do you prefer brut or demi-sec?
responses.phonetic: byan SOOR. voo pray-fay-RAY doo BROOT oo doo duh-mee-SEK ?

responses.french: Voilà, une coupe de champagne. Bonne soirée !
responses.english: Here you are, a glass of champagne. Enjoy your evening!
responses.phonetic: vwah-LAH, oon KOOP duh shahm-PAH-nyuh. bon swah-RAY !

phrases.intent_en: Compliment a performance after the show
phrases.text_local: C'était une représentation extraordinaire. Le ténor était époustouflant !
phrases.pronunciation: say-TAY oon ruh-pray-zan-tah-SYON ex-trah-or-dee-NAIR. luh tay-NOR ay-TAY ay-poos-too-FLAHN !
phrases.cultural_note: 'Époustouflant' (breathtaking / astounding) is a wonderfully expressive French word — using it will delight any Francophile and signal that you truly felt the performance.

phrases.text_local: Qu'est-ce qui est au programme les prochaines semaines ?
phrases.pronunciation: kes-KUH ee ay oh pro-GRAM lay pro-SHEN suh-MEN ?
phrases.cultural_note: 'Au programme' (on the program) is the standard French phrase for what's showing — used for theater, cinema, concerts, and TV. It's a versatile phrase worth knowing.

responses.french: Le mois prochain, nous avons La Traviata et un concert de musique de chambre.
responses.english: Next month we have La Traviata and a chamber music concert.
responses.phonetic: luh mwah pro-SHAN, nooz ah-VOHN lah trah-vyah-TAH ay un kohn-SAIR duh moo-ZEEK duh SHAHM-bruh.

responses.french: Le programme complet est disponible à l'accueil.
responses.english: The full program is available at the front desk.
responses.phonetic: luh pro-GRAM kohm-PLAY ay dees-po-NEE-bluh ah lah-KWHY.
